grantsPool.label: Hunter's Mark — free casts
grantsPool.reminder: Favored Enemy: free cast of Hunter's Mark (no slot).

ref.title: Favored Enemy
body: Hunter's Mark is always prepared, and you can cast it without a spell slot a number of times equal to your Proficiency Bonus per long rest.

ref.title: Deft Explorer
body: You gain Expertise in one skill you're proficient with, and you learn two additional languages.

ref.title: Weapon Mastery
body: You can use the mastery property of two kinds of weapons of your choice; you can change the choices after a long rest. Pick them in the Weapon Mastery panel.

ref.title: Fighting Style
body: You gain a Fighting Style feat of your choice (Archery, Defense, Dueling, Two-Weapon Fighting, etc.) — record it on your sheet.

ref.title: Fighting Style
body: You gain a Fighting Style feat of your choice (Defense, Dueling, Archery, Great Weapon Fighting, etc.) — record it on your sheet.

ref.title: Second Wind
body: As a Bonus Action, regain 1d10 + your fighter level hit points. Regain your uses on a short or long rest.
Tap Use to spend a charge, then roll the d10 and apply it with the Heal control.

ref.title: Weapon Mastery
body: You can use the mastery property of three kinds of weapons of your choice; change the choices after a long rest. Pick them in the Weapon Mastery panel.

ref.title: Action Surge
body: On your turn, take one additional action — most often a second Attack action. Regain it on a short or long rest.

ref.title: Tactical Mind
body: When you fail an ability check, you can expend a use of Second Wind to add 1d10 to the check. If it still fails, you don't lose the Second Wind use.

ref.title: Extra Attack
body: You can attack twice, instead of once, whenever you take the Attack action on your turn.

ref.title: Tactical Shift
body: Whenever you activate Second Wind, you can move up to half your Speed without provoking Opportunity Attacks.

ref.title: Arcane Recovery
body: Once per day when you finish a short rest, recover expended spell slots whose levels total up to half your wizard level (rounded up), none of 6th level or higher.
Tap Use to mark it spent; it returns on a long rest.

ref.title: Ritual Adept
body: You can cast any spell with the Ritual tag as a ritual if it's in your spellbook, without preparing it — it just takes 10 minutes longer and uses no slot.

ref.title: Scholar
body: You gain Expertise in one Intelligence-based skill you're proficient with: Arcana, History, Investigation, Medicine, Nature, or Religion.

ref.title: Memorize Spell
body: Whenever you finish a short rest, you can swap one prepared wizard spell for a different spell from your spellbook.

ref.title: Divine Order
body: Choose Protector — proficiency with martial weapons and heavy armor — or Thaumaturge — an extra cantrip and a bonus to your Intelligence (Arcana/Religion) checks. Record your choice.

ref.title: Channel Divinity
body: A Magic action that fuels a divine option — Divine Spark (heal or radiant/necrotic damage) or Turn Undead. You regain one use on a short rest and all on a long rest.

ref.title: Sear Undead
body: Whenever you use Turn Undead, you also deal radiant damage to each affected undead equal to a roll of your Wisdom modifier in d8s.

ref.title: Expertise
body: Double your Proficiency Bonus on two chosen skill proficiencies (more at higher levels).

ref.title: Sneak Attack
body: Once per turn, deal extra damage (scaling with level) when you hit with a Finesse or Ranged weapon and have Advantage, or an ally is adjacent to the target and you don't have Disadvantage.

ref.title: Thieves' Cant
body: A secret mix of dialect, jargon, and code that lets you hide messages in seemingly normal conversation.

ref.title: Weapon Mastery
body: You gain the mastery property of a number of weapons you're proficient with.

ref.title: Cunning Action
body: A Bonus Action each turn to Dash, Disengage, or Hide.

ref.title: Steady Aim
body: Bonus Action (if you haven't moved): Advantage on your next attack this turn; your speed becomes 0 until end of turn.

ref.title: Cunning Strike
body: When you Sneak Attack, trade dice for effects like Poison, Trip, or Withdraw.

ref.title: Evasion
body: When a Dex save halves damage, you take none on a success and half on a failure.

ref.title: Reliable Talent
body: Treat any d20 of 9 or lower as a 10 on ability checks using a skill you're proficient with.

ref.title: Martial Arts
body: Use Dex for unarmed strikes and monk weapons; roll a Martial Arts die for their damage; make one unarmed strike as a Bonus Action after the Attack action.

ref.title: Unarmored Defense (Monk)
body: While unarmored and not wielding a shield, your AC equals 10 + your Dexterity modifier + your Wisdom modifier.

ref.title: Ki / Monk's Focus
body: Spend Ki to fuel Flurry of Blows, Patient Defense, and Step of the Wind. Regain on a short or long rest.

ref.title: Unarmored Movement
body: Your speed increases while unarmored and not wielding a shield (already in your Speed).

ref.title: Deflect Missiles
body: Reaction when hit by a ranged weapon attack: reduce the damage by 1d10 + Dex + monk level; if reduced to 0, catch and optionally throw it back for 1 Ki.

ref.title: Slow Fall
body: Reaction when you fall: reduce the falling damage by 5 × your monk level.

ref.title: Extra Attack
body: You can attack twice whenever you take the Attack action on your turn.

ref.title: Stunning Strike
body: Spend 1 Ki on a hit to force a Con save or stun the target until your next turn.

ref.title: Ki-Empowered Strikes
body: Your unarmed strikes count as magical for overcoming resistance.

ref.title: Stillness of Mind
body: Action to end one effect on yourself causing you to be Charmed or Frightened.

ref.title: Rage
body: As a Bonus Action, enter a Rage for up to 10 minutes: you gain Resistance to Bludgeoning, Piercing, and Slashing damage, add your Rage Damage bonus to Strength-based melee attacks, and have Advantage on Strength checks and saves.

While not wearing Heavy armor, you can't cast or concentrate on spells; the Rage ends early if you don't attack a hostile creature or take damage by your turn, and you can extend it as a Bonus Action.

ref.title: Unarmored Defense
body: While not wearing armor, your base AC equals 10 + your Dexterity modifier + your Constitution modifier; you can use a Shield and still gain this benefit.

ref.title: Weapon Mastery
body: You can use the mastery property of two kinds of Simple or Martial Melee weapons of your choice, such as Greataxes and Handaxes.
You can change your choices when you finish a Long Rest, and the number of weapons increases as you gain Barbarian levels.

ref.title: Danger Sense
body: You have Advantage on Dexterity saving throws unless you have the Incapacitated condition.
You don't gain this benefit against effects you can't see.

ref.title: Reckless Attack
body: When you make your first attack roll on your turn, you can attack recklessly to gain Advantage on Strength-based melee attack rolls this turn, but attack rolls against you have Advantage until your next turn.

ref.title: Primal Knowledge
body: You gain proficiency in another skill of your choice from the Barbarian list.

While raging, you can channel primal power into certain ability checks, making the following skills use Strength instead of their normal ability: Acrobatics, Intimidation, Perception, Stealth, and Survival.

ref.title: Extra Attack
body: You can attack twice, instead of once, whenever you take the Attack action on your turn.

ref.title: Fast Movement
body: Your Speed increases by 10 feet while you aren't wearing Heavy armor.

ref.title: Bardic Inspiration
body: As a Bonus Action, give a creature within 60 ft (other than yourself) a Bardic Inspiration die (d6). Within 1 hour, before it makes a d20 Test, roll the die and add it to the result. Uses equal your Charisma modifier (min 1), regained on a Long Rest; the die grows to d8 at 5th, d10 at 10th, d12 at 15th.

ref.title: Spellcasting
body: You can cast Bard spells using Charisma as your spellcasting ability. Spell save DC = 8 + Proficiency Bonus + Charisma modifier; spell attack modifier = Proficiency Bonus + Charisma modifier. You prepare a number of leveled spells from the Bard list and may use an Arcane Focus as a Spellcasting Focus.

ref.title: Expertise
body: Choose two of your skill proficiencies to gain Expertise, doubling your Proficiency Bonus for ability checks you make with them. At 9th level you choose two more.

ref.title: Jack of All Trades
body: Add half your Proficiency Bonus (round down) to any ability check you make that doesn't already include your Proficiency Bonus.

ref.title: Font of Inspiration
body: You regain all expended Bardic Inspiration uses on a Short Rest or a Long Rest. You can also expend a spell slot (no action required) to regain one expended use of Bardic Inspiration.

ref.title: Druidic
body: You know Druidic, the secret language of druids, which you can use to leave and decipher hidden messages and which others can't decipher without magic. You always have the Speak with Animals spell prepared.

ref.title: Primal Order
body: Choose Magician or Warden. Magician: you know one extra cantrip from the Druid list, and your Wisdom modifier is added to the Intelligence (Arcana or Nature) checks you make. Warden: you gain Martial weapon proficiency and training with Medium armor.

ref.title: Spellcasting
body: You cast Druid spells using Wisdom, with a focus as a Spellcasting Focus. You know two cantrips and prepare a number of leveled spells from the Druid list, changing your prepared list on a Long Rest.

ref.title: Wild Shape
body: As a Bonus Action you transform into a Beast form you have learned, with stats from the Wild Shape rules, for a number of hours equal to half your druid level. You regain expended uses on a Short or Long Rest, and add forms as you level.

ref.title: Wild Companion
body: You can expend a Wild Shape use to cast Find Familiar without Material components. The familiar is a Fey instead of its normal type and vanishes after a number of hours equal to half your druid level.

ref.title: Wild Resurgence
body: Once per turn you can spend a Wild Shape use to regain one expended Druid spell slot (no higher than level 1). You can also expend a level 1+ spell slot to regain one Wild Shape use, once per Long Rest if you have none left.

ref.title: Lay on Hands
body: You have a pool of healing equal to five times your Paladin level. As a Bonus Action, touch a creature and draw from the pool to restore Hit Points or expend 5 points to neutralize one disease or poison.

ref.title: Spellcasting
body: You prepare and cast Paladin spells using Charisma as your spellcasting ability. You can change your prepared spells when you finish a Long Rest.

ref.title: Weapon Mastery
body: You can use the mastery properties of two kinds of weapons of your choice with which you have proficiency. You can change your choices when you finish a Long Rest.

ref.title: Fighting Style
body: You gain a Fighting Style feat of your choice, or you may instead take the Blessed Warrior feat to learn two Cleric cantrips. You can replace this feat's benefit when you gain a Paladin level.

ref.title: Paladin's Smite
body: You always have the Divine Smite spell prepared, and you can cast it once without a spell slot. You regain the ability to cast it this way on a Long Rest.

ref.title: Channel Divinity
body: You can channel divine energy to fuel magical effects. You have a number of uses equal to your subclass features, regaining one use on a Short Rest and all uses on a Long Rest.

ref.title: Extra Attack
body: You can attack twice, instead of once, whenever you take the Attack action on your turn.

ref.title: Faithful Steed
body: You always have the Find Steed spell prepared, and you can cast it once without a spell slot, regaining that use on a Long Rest. You can also cast it normally using spell slots.

ref.title: Innate Sorcery
body: As a Bonus Action you unleash your innate magic for 1 minute, twice per Long Rest. While active, the save DC of your Sorcerer spells increases by 1 and you have Advantage on attack rolls of the Sorcerer spells you cast.

ref.title: Spellcasting
body: You cast Sorcerer spells using Charisma as your spellcasting ability (DC 8 + proficiency bonus + Charisma modifier). You know a fixed number of cantrips and prepared spells, and cast using Sorcerer spell slots; you can change prepared spells on a Long Rest.

ref.title: Font of Magic
body: You gain Sorcery Points equal to your Sorcerer level (regained on a Long Rest). As a Bonus Action you can convert Sorcery Points into spell slots, or convert spell slots back into Sorcery Points.

ref.title: Metamagic
body: You learn two Metamagic options (e.g. Twinned, Quickened, Subtle, Careful, Distant, Empowered, Heightened, Extended, Seeking, Transmuted Spell). You can use only one Metamagic on a given spell unless noted, spending Sorcery Points to fuel it; you learn more options at higher levels and can swap one on a level-up.

ref.title: Sorcerous Restoration
body: When you finish a Short Rest, you can regain Sorcery Points equal to half your Sorcerer level (rounded down). Once you do so, you can't again until you finish a Long Rest.

ref.title: Eldritch Invocations
body: You learn special magical lore. You start knowing one invocation; you learn more at higher levels, and can replace one whenever you gain a Warlock level.

Options include Pact of the Blade (summon a magic weapon you wield with Charisma), Pact of the Tome (a Book of Shadows granting three cantrips), Pact of the Chain (cast Find Familiar with extra forms), Agonizing Blast (add Charisma modifier to a cantrip's damage), and Devil's Sight (see in normal and magical darkness out to 120 feet).

ref.title: Pact Magic
body: You cast Warlock spells using Charisma. Your Pact Magic spell slots are all the same level and you regain them on a Short or Long Rest.

You know a set number of Warlock spells and cantrips, and can swap one known spell each time you gain a Warlock level. Use a spellcasting focus (such as your weapon or component pouch) for spells with material components.

ref.title: Magical Cunning
body: As a Magic action you can regain expended Pact Magic spell slots (up to half your maximum, round down). Once used, you can't do so again until you finish a Long Rest.
